summary: Founding-docs-only repo to v1 feature-complete in one session. Built two tabs end-to-end on simulator.
completed: Xcode project initialization — Instrument Serif fonts bundled and registered, 13 design tokens as Color Sets in Assets.xcassets (light + dark)
SwiftData models — Note (id, categoryID, text, createdAt, status), Category (id, name, createdAt, sortOrder), NoteStatus enum (active, archived); CloudKit-friendly defaults on every property
ModelContainer wiring with cloudKitDatabase: .automatic via the entitlement; first-launch seed of 'Ideas / TBD' category persisted to UserDefaults under garden.tbd.categoryID
TabView shell — Notes (pencil) + Calm (leaf) with sage tint
NoteComposerView — category picker (Menu), multi-line TextField, sage Add button; empty-trim validation
CategoryChipsView — All chip + dynamic categories + dashed '+' to manage
NoteListView — @Query sorted by createdAt desc, filtered by selected category, in-memory filter (avoids predicate quirks with Codable enums)
NoteRowView — paper card with monospaced caption tags, ink body, archive button
WheatFieldView — TimelineView(.animation) + Canvas, 80 stable @State-seeded blades, sin-based sway with phase offset for traveling-wave effect
OneThingCard — checkbox + italic label, @AppStorage('garden.oneThingCheckedAt') as TimeInterval, isCheckedToday derived via Calendar.current.isDateInToday
ConfettiView — 26 pieces in palette colors (sageDeep/sageSoft/blush/wheat/sky), spring outward + fade over ~1.4s, retriggered via Int counter
Haptics — UIImpactFeedbackGenerator(.medium) on check, .notificationOccurred(.success) chained 0.15s later for confetti
ExportSheet — two-step: markdown preview with category filter + Copy + ShareLink (.md), then archive picker (Archive checked / Keep all active)
ExportMarkdown helper — groups notes by category, sorted by name, formatted dates, includes 'Exported {timestamp}' header

CategoryManagerSheet — List with Bindable rename, EditMode reorder via onMove, swipe-to-delete with confirmation dialog showing note count + 'will move to Ideas / TBD'; Ideas / TBD itself protected via tbdID guard
ArchivedNotesSection — collapsible (chevron + 'Archived (N)' header), embedded inside NoteListView's ScrollView, ArchivedNoteRow with Restore button
App icon — programmatically generated via Python+Pillow, 1024×1024 sage wheat sprig on cream, single universal slot in AppIcon.appiconset
PrivacyInfo.xcprivacy — no tracking, no domains, no collected data types, NSPrivacyAccessedAPICategoryUserDefaults with reason CA92.1
decisions: Removed GardenColors.swift from setup checklist — Xcode 15+ auto-generates Color extensions from asset catalogs, so manual extension causes 'Invalid redeclaration' errors
Path A (Xcode direct install on physical device) recommended before Path B (TestFlight) to validate on real hardware first
TestFlight Internal Testing chosen as distribution path; wife to be added as Internal Tester (no Beta App Review needed)
App icon dark and tinted variants deferred — single universal slot is sufficient for v1 (Apple falls back gracefully)
gotchas: Xcode 15+ auto-generates static vars on Color from asset catalogs — don't write your own extension Color { static let bg = Color('bg') } or you get redeclaration errors per token
SwiftData @Query predicates with Codable enum properties (like NoteStatus) are unreliable in iOS 17 — filter in memory instead
Synchronized folders in Xcode 16 mean files added to disk auto-import; deleting from disk auto-removes from project. No more pbxproj hand-editing
Bundle version in Info.plist references $(CURRENT_PROJECT_VERSION) and $(MARKETING_VERSION), which live in Build Settings — don't edit Info.plist directly
next_session_starting_point: Plug in a real iPhone, ⌘R, validate the app on hardware. Tune wheat field if needed (tunables at top of WheatFieldView.swift: bladeCount, waves, periodSeconds, maxAngleDegrees). After hardware sign-off, begin Apple Developer Program enrollment — that's the gate for everything else (TestFlight, CloudKit production deploy, App Store Connect).

summary: Picked up after compact and shipped the three pre-approved polish items: Settings sheet exposing the constitution-required 'Export everything' path, dark + tinted app icon variants, and a full App Store Connect listing copy doc.
completed: SettingsView.swift — sheet presented from a new gear icon on Notes top-leading toolbar (share button moved to trailing). Three cards: About (app name, tagline, version from Info.plist), Storage ('Export everything' with archived-toggle, Copy via UIPasteboard, ShareLink for full markdown), and a sage-tinted philosophy card explaining the three-layer storage model in user terms.
App icon dark variant — ink-dark bg (#1A1F18) + cream-light stem (#E8E0D0), generated by /tmp/generate_garden_icons.py
App icon tinted variant — black bg + white stem so iOS 18 luminance tinting renders the silhouette in the user's chosen home-screen tint
AppIcon.appiconset/Contents.json now declares all three appearances (universal, luminosity:dark, luminosity:tinted); old icon.png removed

app-store-listing.md — name, subtitle, promo text, ~2.1k-char description, keywords, support/marketing/privacy URL guidance, hosted-ready privacy policy draft, nutrition-label answer ('Data Not Collected'), age rating, category, copyright, 'What to Test' notes for TestFlight, tester invite email body, post-upload ASC checklist, and screenshots backlog with suggested 5-shot set
decisions: Settings is a sheet on Notes, not a third tab — preserves the constitution's two-tab lock and keeps Calm screen single-purpose
Icon variants generated programmatically rather than hand-designed — same sprig in three palettes, consistent and regenerable; hand-designed icon stays on the v2 backlog
App Store listing copy lives in-repo as markdown so it versions with the app; ASC becomes a paste target, not a composition surface

Description text leans into the anti-patterns ('no notifications, no streaks') rather than softening for App Review — Apple does not require engagement features and the constitution's stance is load-bearing
gotchas: iOS 18 tinted-icon variant uses luminance — the system tints based on brightness of your image, so a black bg + white silhouette gives the cleanest result. A multi-color image would muddy the tint.
Two-tab constitutional lock is easy to forget when adding a 'natural' surface like Settings; reach for a sheet, not a TabView entry

App Store Connect's 'What to Test' field is per-build, but the bulk listing copy (description, keywords, etc.) is per-app — separating them in the markdown doc avoids re-pasting on every TestFlight build
next_session_starting_point: Real-device smoke test (Path A: plug iPhone, ⌘R), then the deployment chain — CloudKit Dashboard 'Deploy Schema Changes' for container iCloud.com.drivercyber.garden → ASC record creation at appstoreconnect.apple.com (bundle ID com.drivercyber.garden) → Xcode Product → Archive → Distribute → App Store Connect → Upload → add wife as Internal Tester → paste 'What to Test' copy from app-store-listing.md.

summary: Honest code review of the full v1 surface (~1,300 lines, 20 Swift files). Found two CloudKit blockers that would have shipped a non-syncing app to TestFlight, plus minor cleanups. Fixed all three immediately-actionable items.
completed: gardenApp.swift — added cloudKitDatabase: .automatic to ModelConfiguration. SwiftData was previously configured for local-only persistence despite the iCloud capability being on.
garden.entitlements — added 'iCloud.com.drivercyber.garden' to com.apple.developer.icloud-container-identifiers (was an empty array). Without this, the entitlement was effectively decorative.
Item.swift — deleted. Default Xcode template's @Model class Item was never in the Schema, never referenced — dead code.
decisions: Entitlement-on + container-empty + ModelConfiguration-without-cloudKitDatabase is a silent failure mode: simulator runs fine, sync just doesn't happen. Real-device users with a second Apple device would be the first to notice.

When CloudKit-mirrored SwiftData is required, the cloudKitDatabase parameter on ModelConfiguration is load-bearing. The .modelContainer(...) scene-modifier form makes this hard to miss; the hand-rolled ModelContainer init makes it easy to miss.

Deferred (not blocking TestFlight): consolidate UserDefaults string keys into a DefaultsKeys enum; demote @Bindable to let on NoteRowView; consider seeding synchronously to avoid the first-launch race window.
gotchas: ModelConfiguration(schema:isStoredInMemoryOnly:) defaults cloudKitDatabase to .none. The CloudKit capability + entitlement do NOT imply CloudKit-backed SwiftData — the configuration must explicitly opt in.
An iCloud entitlement with an empty container-identifiers array is a no-op. Apple does not warn at build time; the failure surfaces only when users observe missing sync.

The CloudKit Dashboard 'Deploy Schema Changes' button has nothing to deploy until the app has run at least once with a working CloudKit configuration. Run-once-then-deploy is the correct order; deploying first does nothing useful.
Xcode 16's synchronized folders auto-update the project file when source files are added or removed on disk — no need to hand-edit project.pbxproj after deleting Item.swift.
next_session_starting_point: Run the app once on simulator (or real device) so SwiftData registers the now-correct CloudKit dev schema. Then proceed with the original plan: real-device smoke test (Path A) → CloudKit Dashboard 'Deploy Schema Changes' → ASC record → Archive + Upload → TestFlight Internal Tester invites. A scheduled check-in cron is set for 2026-04-28 09:57 PDT to ping on TestFlight progress (session-only, may not survive a Claude restart).

session: real-device install + polish pass + App-Store-first pivot
summary: Long single sitting that spanned three threads: (1) got Garden onto Chad's iPhone 16e via Path A after debugging deployment-target mismatch, codesign trust, dyld extraction, and disk-space exhaustion; (2) ran a polish pass driven by Chad's live feedback while testing on the device; (3) Chad reframed the distribution strategy mid-session — public App Store submission ASAP for the practice rep, then a 1.1 update before sharing with wife/friends.
completed: Path A real-device install — chained through iOS deployment-target mismatch (lowered 26.4 → 17.6), Mac keychain codesign authorization, 'Trust This Computer' on iPhone, iOS Developer Mode, and finally a disk-space cleanup (cleared ~836MB DerivedData + ~6.6GB iOS DeviceSupport, freed disk from 10GB to 18GB) which unblocked dyld_shared_cache_extract_dylibs. App now runs on Chad's iPhone 16e.
NoteComposerView — keyboard 'Done' toolbar item (ToolbarItemGroup placement: .keyboard) dismisses @FocusState; addNote() also drops focus on save
NoteListView — searchQuery parameter, case-insensitive substring filter on note.text, scrollDismissesKeyboard(.interactively), empty-state messaging that adapts to the query
NotesView — searchable(text:placement:.navigationBarDrawer(displayMode: .automatic), prompt:) hooks the system search bar into the navigation chrome

NoteRowView — inline edit mode: pencil button toggles isEditing @State, TextField bound to a draft, Save (writes trimmed text back to note) and Cancel (discards) buttons; existing Archive button preserved alongside
ExportSheet archive picker — Dictionary(grouping: by:) with Category? sort by sortOrder; renders monospaced caption headers above each section instead of a flat list

OneThingCard — full vertical redesign matching the original web composition: 'Breathe.' (Instrument Serif italic 44pt) headline, 'One thing. One breath. One check.' subheadline, 96pt rounded-square checkbox (sageDeep fill + paper checkmark when checked, bg fill + line stroke when not), italic serif 'Tap when you've done the one thing.' footer; entire card stays tappable via Button wrapper

WheatFieldView — per-blade shadeIndex into a 5-shade palette generated at draw time via UIColor HSB extraction with brightness offsets [-0.20, -0.10, 0, +0.08, +0.16]; respects light/dark Color.wheat asset variants
ConfettiView — replaced .spring(0.7, 0.55) with .easeOut(duration: 1.6) so pieces drift instead of fling; piece-removal deadline 1.4s → 2.6s

CalmView — OneThingCard bottom padding 200 → 160 → 300 across iterations after Chad sketched the desired position on a screenshot; final value lands the card in the upper-middle of the screen with the wheat field a clear backdrop
Memory — saved feedback_two_tab_simplicity.md ('Notes + Calm only; secondary surfaces go through Settings/menu') and rewrote project_distribution_goal.md to reflect the App-Store-first pivot
DECISIONS.md — added 2026-04-26 entry (Decisions 17, 18, 19); current-phase block rewritten to reflect submission gates rather than engineering gates

garden-app-tracker.html — restructured priorities for App Store path (CloudKit deploy → privacy policy hosting → screenshots → ASC record → archive upload → 1.0/1.1 cadence); JSON tracker-data block kept in sync
decisions: Distribution pivots from TestFlight Internal first to public App Store first, for the practice rep — Chad wants to learn ASC submission, App Review wait, and metadata-rejection patterns on a personal low-stakes app. Friends never see 1.0; they see 1.1.

Two-tab simplicity is now an explicit, locked feedback memory — secondary surfaces (reporting, recap, exports) route through Settings or contextual menus, never as new top-level tabs. Treat any 'add a tab' suggestion as a constitution-level change.
Polish-pass commits stand alone (one coherent slice each); doc updates land in their own commit so diffs are reviewable as documentation. Going-forward default.

CloudKit deploy paused intentionally while Chad collects polish feedback on the device; the deploy is still required before any TestFlight or App Store build, but isn't blocking dev-build testing on his iPhone.
gotchas: iOS deployment target ≠ iOS SDK target — Chad's iPhone on 26.3.1 couldn't install builds compiled against 26.4 even though Xcode was newer than the device. Lowering Minimum Deployments to 17.6 fixed it; modern iOS APIs the app actually uses are all 17+.

dyld_shared_cache_extract_dylibs failure on first device install was a disk-space symptom, not a Developer Mode or Xcode-version symptom. Always check Available Storage before going down the codesign / Developer Mode rabbit hole.

ENOSPC on rm -rf ~/Library/Developer/Xcode/DerivedData itself means the disk is so full that even unlinking files needs free space for the journal — clear smaller things first (iOS DeviceSupport, then DerivedData chunks).

CloudKit Console dev → production deploy: dev builds auto-connect to Development (auto-creates schema), TestFlight + App Store builds connect to Production. The 'Deploy Schema Changes' button location is hidden in the redesigned Console — likely behind Settings, per-environment view, or per-record-type action menu.

When SwiftUI .searchable is placed on a NavigationStack child, .navigationBarDrawer(displayMode: .automatic) integrates with the nav chrome and gives the iOS-native pull-down behavior. Other placements render the bar in unexpected spots.

@FocusState property on a parent view + .focused($state) on a TextField + a ToolbarItemGroup(.keyboard) item that flips the state to false is the cleanest way to do programmatic keyboard dismissal in SwiftUI. .scrollDismissesKeyboard(.interactively) layers on swipe-to-dismiss on top.

Dictionary(grouping: by:) returns an unordered dictionary; always sort the resulting key→array pairs explicitly before .map-ing into ForEach, otherwise section ordering is non-deterministic across runs.

Color.wheat → UIColor → getHue/saturation/brightness/alpha → adjusted UIColor → Color is a clean way to derive shade variants from a semantic asset color while preserving its dark-mode variant. Cache the palette per draw, not per blade.
next_session_starting_point: Chad signed off the polish pass on 2026-04-26 — 'I love it! The core functions are there.' The 8 local commits are pushed to origin/main and the next session opens against App Store submission gates, not the codebase: (1) hunt the CloudKit Dashboard 'Deploy Schema Changes' button in the redesigned Console — try Settings sidebar, per-environment view, or per-record-type action menus; (2) host the privacy policy (draft in app-store-listing.md → GitHub Pages or published iCloud note); (3) capture 5 screenshots at 6.7" iPhone size from the device or simulator; (4) create the ASC record and fill in the App Privacy nutrition label as 'Data Not Collected'; (5) Archive + Upload from Xcode; (6) submit for App Review. After 1.0 ships, fold anything Chad notices into 1.1 and only then share the link with wife/friends.

session: manifest deletion (Plan B), tracker creation, CloudKit verification deferred
summary: Build 4 came back rejected with the same ITMS-91056. Three rejections of three syntactically-valid minimal manifests pointed to the validator choking on the file's existence rather than its content. Deleted PrivacyInfo.xcprivacy entirely; build 5 archived and submitted, awaiting Apple. Created garden-app-tracker.html for the project-dashboard, committed to main as 540e3df. Started CloudKit verification but blocked on a recordName-not-queryable Console gotcha — deferred verification, captured the gotcha in DECISIONS.md.
completed: Verified no SPM dependencies bundling competing privacy manifests (find -name '*.xcprivacy' returned only the one source file + its bundled copies)
Deleted garden-app/garden/PrivacyInfo.xcprivacy — Xcode 16 PBXFileSystemSynchronizedRootGroup auto-syncs the deletion, no project-file edit required
Created garden-app-tracker.html at repo root with three-priority JSON payload, committed and pushed standalone (540e3df) so the project-dashboard at garden.chadstewartcpa.com sees it immediately

DECISIONS.md — Current Phase rewritten for build 5 / manifest-deleted state; added 2026-04-27 entry with Decisions 24 (manifest deleted) and 25 (tracker location); added new 'Known Operational Gotchas' section
Memory project_distribution_goal.md — full rewrite for the deleted-manifest state; CloudKit recordName-queryable gotcha encoded as future-session knowledge
Tracker priorities updated to four items reflecting current state (build 5 wait, CloudKit verification, TestFlight, 1.1 polish)
decisions: Privacy manifest deleted entirely as Plan B from the fallback ladder. Three identical rejections of three different valid configurations meant Apple was choking on the file's existence (with empty arrays) rather than its content. Deletion is constitutionally aligned with 'we declare nothing because we collect nothing' since Garden uses no required-reason APIs.

If build 5 also gets ITMS-91056, escalate to Apple Developer Support — at that point we have a clean reproduction (no manifest in bundle at all) which is much stronger evidence than 'we keep tweaking the file and Apple keeps rejecting'.

Project-dashboard tracker (garden-app-tracker.html) lives at repo root, not nested. Committed standalone so the dashboard sees it without waiting for the bundled submission-run commit. Future session-end protocol updates this file's priorities array to match what's actually in flight.

Don't recreate PrivacyInfo.xcprivacy without Apple specifically asking — if build 5 validates, the constitutional claim about 'minimum-viable disclosures' (Decision 9) is best honored by no disclosure when nothing applies.
gotchas: CloudKit Console queries require recordName to be a Queryable index, even when predicating on other fields — CloudKit's query mechanism uses recordName internally for pagination. SwiftData record types (CD_Note) ship with no recordName index by default. Fix: Schema → Indexes → + → recordName Queryable. One-time per environment.

App Store auto-validator can reject the *existence* of a privacy manifest, not just its contents. Three identical ITMS-91056 rejections of three different valid minimal manifests pointed to either empty-array intolerance or cached-state comparison; deletion was the only resolution. Don't assume an ITMS-91056 means the file's contents are wrong.
Xcode 16's PBXFileSystemSynchronizedRootGroup auto-syncs folder deletions just like additions — `rm` the file from disk and the next build excludes it from the bundle, no project-file edit needed.
next_session_starting_point: Build 5 was submitted at session end; Apple's email lands within 30–60 minutes if it's a rejection or stays silent for 1–3 days if processing succeeds. Open Mail / ASC → Activity → Build 5 first thing. If green: attach build 5 to v1.0, the existing 'Waiting for Review' submission re-validates, and we wait on actual App Review. If red with another ITMS-91056: open an Apple Developer Support ticket — we've now executed the full fallback ladder. Independent of build status, finish the CloudKit verification: Schema → Indexes → + → CD_Note, recordName, Queryable; then Records → CD_Note query in Private Database to confirm notes are in iCloud before the dev-build → App Store install transition. Submission-run config diffs (pbxproj iPad drop, Info.plist encryption exemption, DECISIONS.md updates, deleted PrivacyInfo.xcprivacy) are still uncommitted — bundle them with the build-5 build-number bump in one '1.0 submission run' commit when the dust settles.

summary: Build 5 (manifest-deleted, Plan B) was accepted by Apple's validator and approved by App Review ~23 hours after submission. Garden is live at https://apps.apple.com/app/garden-notes-calm/id6763959626 under the name 'Garden – Notes & Calm'. The submission-ergonomics fight is over.
completed: 1.0 submission run committed (ef416a8) — iPad dropped from TARGETED_DEVICE_FAMILY, ITSAppUsesNonExemptEncryption=false in Info.plist, PrivacyInfo.xcprivacy deleted, build number bumped to 5
Build 5 archived and uploaded; Apple validator passed (first acceptance after 3 prior ITMS-91056 rejections on builds 2–4)
App Review approved overnight; approval email timestamped 2026-04-28 04:53 AM PT, submission timestamped 2026-04-27 06:03 AM PT (~23h end-to-end)
App live at https://apps.apple.com/app/garden-notes-calm/id6763959626
decisions: Plan B (manifest deletion) is the confirmed answer for ITMS-91056 when an app has no required-reason API usage — promoted from 'tried' to 'works'.
App name on the listing is 'Garden – Notes & Calm', the longer alternate from app-store-listing.md. 'Garden' alone was presumably unavailable at submission time.
gotchas: Once the validator finally accepts a build, App Review itself is fast — ~23 hours for a first submission with a clean validator pass. The multi-day pain was entirely the pre-review validator, not the human review queue.

App Store Connect's 'Waiting for Review' submission auto-re-validates against any new build attached to the same version; you don't need to re-submit the metadata, just upload a new build and ASC picks it up.
next_session_starting_point: 1.0 is live. Two open threads: (1) 1.1 polish — collect anything that surfaced post-launch and ship before sending the link to wife/friends (per Decision 17). (2) CloudKit verification still deferred from 2026-04-27 — Schema → Indexes → + CD_Note recordName Queryable, then query Private Database to confirm notes are syncing in production. Independent of 1.1 since the dev → prod schema deploy needs to happen before any 1.1 submission anyway.

summary: Doc close-out for 1.0 approval committed (b570a88), tracker JSON reshaped to the project-dashboard's expected schema and committed direct to main (f91e0c9), feature branch tracker pre-aligned to avoid merge conflict (f6900c0), feature branch merged into main as aa7dae2. App Store post-launch update workflow walked through as background for the upcoming description rewrite + 1.1 polish.
completed: DECISIONS.md / learned-log.json / garden-app-tracker.html updated for 1.0 LIVE state (commit b570a88)
ITMS-91056 gotcha hardened with build-5 confirmation; Decision 26 promoting Plan B from 'tried' to 'works' added to DECISIONS.md
garden-app-tracker.html reshaped to the dashboard's columns/priorities/backlog schema (commit f91e0c9 on main)
Feature branch's tracker file pre-aligned to main's schema to avoid merge conflict (commit f6900c0)
Feature branch claude/review-app-store-session-NJ1N1 merged into main as aa7dae2 — DECISIONS.md and learned-log.json close-out is now on main
Decision 27 added to DECISIONS.md encoding the tracker JSON schema; CLAUDE.md §What This Repo Contains updated with a pointer to it
decisions: garden-app-tracker.html uses columns[].priorities[].{title, note} + backlog[] schema (Decision 27). Future §Session-End Protocol just edits the priorities/backlog content, not the structure.
Doc-only commits and dashboard fixes can go direct-to-main; the feature-branch + merge dance is reserved for code changes that warrant review surface.
gotchas: When a project-dashboard tracker fetches but renders incomplete, the JSON shape is the first thing to check — the card renderer is strict and silently degrades to description-only if columns/priorities/backlog are missing.

git checkout main on a stale local branch can show DECISIONS.md (or any file) as 'modified' in system reminders even though nothing was edited — it's just the branch difference. Don't revert; fast-forward instead.

ASC fields split into three categories: editable-anytime (Promotional Text 170 chars, pricing, availability), per-version-with-review (description, keywords, subtitle, screenshots, What's New), and per-build (binary code, MARKETING_VERSION, CURRENT_PROJECT_VERSION). Promotional Text is the freshness lever between formal updates.

Subsequent App Reviews are typically faster than the first — 4–12 hours is common once the validator state is settled and reviewers focus on what changed. Apple's published median is ~24h for 50% of submissions, ~48h for 90%.
next_session_starting_point: 1.0 is live and stable. Two threads still open: (1) 1.1 polish — Chad will collect post-launch notes from his own usage, draft a new description paragraph, and bundle them into the next submission. The Promotional Text field can be edited in ASC anytime without review for fresh launch copy. (2) CloudKit verification still deferred — Schema → Indexes → + CD_Note recordName Queryable, then query Private Database to confirm sync in production. Required before any 1.1 submission. Tracker schema is now correct on main; no further dashboard fixes anticipated.

session: tracker shipped[] enrichment + Xcode Cloud awareness (evening)
summary: Short follow-on. Enriched the project-dashboard tracker with a 14-entry shipped[] array reconstructed from git log so the dashboard's Galaxy tab can render dates and tags. Surfaced from a build-success email that Xcode Cloud is actively building on every main push — even doc-only commits trigger an Archive – iOS run.
completed: garden-app-tracker.html — added shipped[] inside columns[0]: 14 objects {date, what, tags[], learned?} sorted newest-first (2026-04-28 → 2026-04-25), JSON validated, committed direct-to-main as 0d2ed39
DECISIONS.md — Decision 28 encoded as the shipped[] schema extension to Decision 27; added Xcode Cloud auto-build gotcha to §Known Operational Gotchas
CLAUDE.md §What This Repo Contains — tracker line extended to mention the shipped[] schema and Decision 28 alongside Decision 27
decisions: Tracker shipped[] items use {date, what, tags[], learned?} object shape, sorted newest-first; dates from git log when reconstructing history, omit-rather-than-guess for unknowns; tags come from the shared cross-project dashboard vocab where possible.
Future §Session-End Protocol appends the day's wins to shipped[] in object form (with date + tags) — don't rewrite older entries when their dates were best-guess.
gotchas: Xcode Cloud auto-builds on every push to main — even doc-only commits trigger Archive – iOS. The build-success email subject is 'garden -- Build N succeeded (main)'. To skip a build, push to a non-main branch. To stop builds entirely, edit the workflow trigger in App Store Connect → Xcode Cloud.

An Xcode Cloud success email reading 'Results: Archive - iOS' tells you the build compiled and archived but doesn't tell you whether it shipped to TestFlight. TestFlight delivery requires a 'TestFlight (Internal Testing)' post-action on the workflow; absent that, the archive sits in Xcode Cloud and goes nowhere. To verify: open TestFlight on the iPhone after a push and see if the build appears within ~10–20 min.

Reconstructing a shipped[] array from git log: `git log --format='%cs %h %s' main` is the cleanest source; group multiple commits on the same day into one shipped item when they form a coherent slice (e.g. a single polish pass).
next_session_starting_point: 1.0 is live; tracker now has full priorities/backlog/shipped data for the dashboard's Galaxy tab. Open threads unchanged from the afternoon entry: (1) 1.1 polish + description rewrite for the next submission, and (2) CloudKit verification (CD_Note recordName Queryable index) before that submission. Optional infra task: at the next Mac session, check whether the Xcode Cloud Default workflow has a TestFlight post-action attached — if yes, doc-only commits will continue producing TestFlight builds (waste of build numbers); if no, attach one and we have a real remote-iteration loop. Either way, prefer pushing tracker/doc-only changes to a side branch when at the Mac to keep main's build numbers tied to actual code changes.

session: v2 build day — widgets, App Group store, Garden Inbox Shortcut, Calm/composer polish
summary: Marathon build session that took Garden from v1.0 (notes + Calm only) to v2-feature-complete: Home Screen dashboard widget, Lock Screen circular + rectangular accessories, App Group SwiftData store with one-time migration, AddToGardenInboxIntent App Intent + Garden Inbox user Shortcut (Speak/Type) distributed via iCloud share link with one-tap installer in Settings, breath-mimicking Calm checkbox animation, painterly multi-tone wheat field with chunkier stalks, Inbox-as-default category with idempotent dedup pass against CloudKit-restored duplicates, ViewThatFits chip overflow, keyboard-toolbar Add. Everything compiled clean across both targets; Chad is testing on device. v2 unblocks the friends/family share once Chad signs off and CloudKit prod schema is deployed.
completed: Widget extension target wired (Chad created in Xcode, Claude wrote all the Swift): GardenWidget bundle with one Widget supporting .systemMedium (dashboard), .accessoryCircular (quick compose), and .accessoryRectangular (Inbox count). TimelineProvider reads count from shared UserDefaults; main app writes via InboxCountStore.refresh + WidgetCenter.reloadAllTimelines.

App Group SwiftData store: GardenStoreLocator helper resolves the shared store URL at group.com.drivercyber.garden/Garden.sqlite. One-time migration finds any *.store file at the default Application Support location and copies it (with -shm and -wal sidecars) to the App Group container. Idempotent — fresh installs skip; subsequent launches skip.

Cross-process inbox identity: garden.inbox.categoryID lives in App Group UserDefaults so the App Intent process can read it. seedIfNeeded writes to shared defaults; intent + InboxCountStore + NoteComposerView all read from shared defaults via GardenStoreLocator.sharedDefaults.

AddToGardenInboxIntent (App Intent): @Parameter Note with requestValueDialog 'What's on your mind?', openAppWhenRun: false, opens its own ModelContainer at the App Group URL, inserts a Note in the Inbox category, calls InboxCountStore.refresh, returns .result(dialog: 'Saved to Inbox.'). Registered via GardenShortcuts: AppShortcutsProvider with Siri phrases and shortTitle 'Add to Inbox'.

Garden Inbox user Shortcut: Choose-from-Menu (Speak/Type) → Dictate Text or Ask for Input → Add to Garden Inbox with Menu Result as the note text. Published as iCloud share link https://www.icloud.com/shortcuts/a7c75ea192d244c8bb7f17ee2fa7d29c.
Settings 'Quick capture' card with one-tap install button (Link to the iCloud share URL) + Action Button setup tip. New users tap install → Shortcuts.app shows import sheet → 'Add Shortcut' → done.

Widget tap routing: Compose buttons (dashboard + lock screen circular) use Link(destination: shortcuts://run-shortcut?name=Garden%20Inbox), which opens Shortcuts.app for the parameter prompt then runs silently. Calm button uses Link(destination: garden://calm).

garden:// URL scheme registered in Info.plist (CFBundleURLTypes); ContentView handles calm/notes/compose hosts. compose posts a NotificationCenter signal (.gardenFocusComposer) that NoteComposerView observes to set isComposerFocused = true after a small tab-switch delay.

Category dedup pass: reconcile() runs in seedIfNeeded + every scenePhase=.active. Groups categories by name, keeps the oldest (by createdAt), reassigns notes from duplicates to the keeper, deletes duplicates, saves once. Also re-resolves the Inbox ID into shared defaults so the intent always points at the survivor.

Inbox seed simplified to idempotent name lookup: 'Inbox' created only if no category by that name exists. Sort order set to (min existing) - 1 so it lands first. Legacy 'Ideas / TBD' no longer seeded but its ID is still preserved for users who already have one.

Composer ↔ chip filter coupling: NoteComposerView takes a filterCategoryID prop; .onChange(of: filterCategoryID) sets draftCategoryID = new so chip taps double as composer category switching. resolvedCategoryID falls back chain: draft → filter → defaultInbox → defaultTBD → first.

ViewThatFits chip overflow in CategoryChipsView: full strip when chips fit; compact strip ([All] [Inbox always pinned] [<selected if not Inbox>] [Categories ▼]) when they don't. No horizontal scrolling either way.
Composer cleanup: Add button moved to the keyboard toolbar (Done on left, Add on right with .bold and disabled state). Inline Add at bottom of composer removed entirely.

Calm screen breath animation: tap unchecked One Thing → light haptic → 0.9s easeOut inflate to scale 1.22 + checkmark fades in → at peak medium haptic → 0.6s easeIn deflate to 1.0 + confetti burst + 0.35s later success haptic. Confetti spawns from a stationary origin (not scaled).

Wheat field redesign: blade widths 3–6pt (was 2–4pt), 5-tone palette varying hue (-0.018 to +0.030), saturation (0.65–1.10×), brightness (0.78–1.05×) of Color.wheat — amber, standard, pale, golden, olive-leaning. Painterly multi-tone field instead of single-hue brightness ramp.

Garden palette colorsets (bg, paper, ink, ink2, ink3, line, sageDeep, sageSoft, sageTint, wheat) copied into GardenWidget/Assets.xcassets so widget can resolve Color.bg, Color.sageDeep, etc. at build time.
Keyboard toolbar Done button moved to the left in NoteComposerView so it stops overlapping the (then-still-present, now-removed) inline Add.
decisions: Widget tap → shortcuts://run-shortcut?name=... is the chosen pattern for any quick-capture flow that needs a parameter prompt. Direct Button(intent:) is non-conversational and silently fails on requestValueDialog regardless of openAppWhenRun.

App Group SwiftData store + matching CloudKit capability on the widget extension target is the chosen infra for cross-process intent writes that sync correctly. Avoid mixing cloudKitDatabase: .none (extension) with .automatic (main app) on the same store — they fight over CloudKit metadata.

AddToGardenInboxIntent.openAppWhenRun = false. Writes happen silently from the extension process; success is signaled by .result(dialog:) overlay in Shortcuts.app, not by foregrounding Garden. Aligns with the constitution's 'Garden must earn opens; never demands them' principle.
Inbox is the de-facto default category in v2; Ideas/TBD lives on for legacy users but is no longer seeded for new installs.

Distribute the polished Garden Inbox Shortcut (Choose-from-Menu Speak/Type) via iCloud share link + Settings installer instead of trying to reproduce its UI inside an App Intent — App Intents can't natively show menus + dictation choices.
ViewThatFits is the chip overflow pattern (no horizontal ScrollView). Inbox is the auto-pinned chip in the compact strip; user-controlled per-category pinning is parking lot.
Reconcile dedup pass runs on every launch + every foreground; cheap and idempotent. Don't try to prevent CloudKit-restored duplicates with timing tricks — clean them up after the fact.

The 'Saved to Inbox' overlay after a Shortcut run is system-level Shortcuts.app feedback, not a Garden push notification — different mechanism, doesn't violate the constitution's 'no notifications' anti-pattern.
gotchas: Button(intent:) inside widgets / lock-screen accessories runs the intent in a non-conversational background context. requestValueDialog NEVER fires there, even with openAppWhenRun: true. The widget framework simply doesn't surface parameter prompts. Workaround: Link to a shortcuts://run-shortcut URL so Shortcuts.app hosts the prompt.

Switching the SwiftData store path (default Application Support → App Group container) requires migrating the existing local database including its -wal and -shm sidecars. Without copying the sidecars, you can lose recent unmerged writes from the WAL log.

Widget extension target needs BOTH App Group capability AND CloudKit capability (with the same iCloud container ID as the main app) for silent intent writes that sync. App Group alone gives you the shared store but cloudKitDatabase: .automatic will fail at runtime without CloudKit entitlement on the extension.

Xcode 16's PBXFileSystemSynchronizedRootGroup auto-includes new Swift files in the owning target — no project.pbxproj edits needed for files in the synchronized folder. But cross-target shared files (e.g. AddToGardenInboxIntent shared between main app and widget extension) still need manual Target Membership ticks via File Inspector → Target Membership.

Asset catalogs are per-target. Using Color.bg / Color.sageDeep / etc. in the widget required physically copying the .colorset directories from the main app's Assets.xcassets into the widget's Assets.xcassets — Xcode auto-generates Color extensions per-target from each catalog.
Adding the iCloud capability shell to a target is not enough — must also tick the CloudKit checkbox under Services AND tick the specific container under Containers. Easy to miss.

AppShortcutsProvider auto-registers an App Shortcut as an *action* (visible in Spotlight, Siri, and the Shortcuts action picker) but does NOT auto-create a user Shortcut card in the Shortcuts library. iOS sometimes can't even find the auto-registered shortcut by name via shortcuts:// URL.

iCloud share link generation in Shortcuts.app requires iCloud Drive enabled (Settings → Apple ID → iCloud → iCloud Drive ON), separate from being signed in to iCloud generally. Misleading 'sign in to iCloud' alert if iCloud Drive is off even when the account is signed in.

SwiftData + CloudKit doesn't enforce uniqueness at the schema level (CloudKit doesn't support .unique constraints). Any 'seed by name lookup' approach will race with the initial CloudKit sync on a fresh install with prior iCloud data. Robust pattern: periodic dedup pass keyed on the chosen field.

CFPrefsPlistSource warning ('Using kCFPreferencesAnyUser with a container is only allowed for System Containers') and NSKeyedUnarchiveFromData deprecation warning are benign system noise when using App Group UserDefaults / SwiftData internals. Apple's framework noise — not actionable on our side.

openAppWhenRun: true does NOT make widget Button(intent:) parameter prompts appear. Misleading flag — only controls whether the app foregrounds AFTER the intent runs; doesn't change the parameter resolution context.
next_session_starting_point: v2 is feature-complete and Chad is testing on device. Next session opens with: (1) any post-test fix-ups Chad reports back from the build day's massive ship list — widget Compose flow (silent capture via Garden Inbox Shortcut), lock screen accessories, Calm breath animation timing, painterly wheat colors, chip overflow + pinned Inbox, dedup behavior on his existing duplicates; (2) CloudKit production schema deploy (CloudKit Console → Schema → Indexes → +CD_Note recordName Queryable → Deploy to Production) — same long-deferred gate, now blocking v2 submission; (3) v2 App Store submission with description rewrite emphasizing widget + lock screen + Action Button capture; (4) share link with wife/friends after submission goes live. Backlog candy: hand-designed app icon, Quarterly Recap v2 word-cloud, seasonal Calm variants, per-category pinning UI in Manage Categories. Cleanup carryover: trim CLAUDE.md §Session-End Protocol to link chad-wiki instead of inlining the body.

session: v2 polish — Inbox-as-gated-feature + listing copy draft (PM)
summary: Chad came back from his first device-test cycle of the v2 build with three asks: (1) submit v2, (2) link the Shortcut to widget/lock-screen buttons, (3) gate the Inbox category behind Shortcut install. Read 2+3 as the same idea — the Garden Inbox Shortcut is the gating mechanism for the entire Inbox feature, not just a quick-capture path. Implemented strict gating: a single App Group flag (garden.inboxEnabled) controls whether the Inbox category appears in the chip strip, composer dropdown, widget count line, and lock screen rectangular accessory. Two flip paths: Settings install button tap (optimistic — assume install) and AddToGardenInboxIntent run (definitive proof). Either path also lazily creates the Inbox category. New garden://setup-inbox + garden://settings deep links route widget/lock-screen Compose taps to the Settings sheet when the gate is off. Drafted v2 App Store listing copy reflecting widgets + lock screen + gated Inbox; surfaced for Chad's review.
completed: InboxGate helper (Helpers/InboxGate.swift): isEnabled accessor reads garden.inboxEnabled from App Group defaults; enable(in:) flips the flag, lazily creates the Inbox category at sortOrder min-1, writes the inbox ID to shared defaults, calls InboxCountStore.refresh (which reloads widgets), posts .gardenInboxEnabled.

Removed Inbox auto-seed from gardenApp.seedIfNeeded — fresh installs now have zero categories until the user installs the Shortcut. reconcile() now also removes the inbox.categoryID from shared defaults if no Inbox exists, so widgets don't show stale state.

NotesView gates the chip strip + composer category dropdown via a visibleCategories computed prop (filters out Inbox when !inboxEnabled). selectedID is wrapped in a custom binding so a hidden selection silently falls back to 'All'. State is refreshed on .onAppear, scenePhase=.active (cross-process intent path), and .gardenInboxEnabled notification (in-process Settings tap path).

NoteComposerView's resolvedCategoryID now validates each fallback ID against the visible categories list — defaultInboxID is skipped if Inbox isn't visible. Prevents 'ghost' selections after the gate flips off.

SettingsView Quick Capture card converted from a Link to a Button: taps call InboxGate.enable(in: modelContext) THEN openURL to the iCloud share URL. Card UI now reflects gate state — title flips between 'Speak or type to Inbox' (off) and 'Inbox is set up' (on), button label flips between Install and Reinstall, an 'Enabled ✓' chip appears when on. Listens to .gardenInboxEnabled to update if the intent flips it.

AddToGardenInboxIntent.perform() calls InboxGate.enable(in: context) before the save. Catches users who side-load the Shortcut without going through the Settings install button, and is the definitive 'Shortcut is installed' signal.

ContentView extended with garden://setup-inbox + garden://settings deep links; both switch to Notes tab and post .gardenShowSettings (NotesView listens and presents the Settings sheet). Notification names consolidated in ContentView (gardenFocusComposer, gardenInboxEnabled, gardenShowSettings).

GardenWidget refactored to read garden.inboxEnabled from shared defaults into GardenEntry. When off: dashboard Compose Link routes to garden://setup-inbox with subtitle 'set up Inbox', dashboard count line becomes a Tap to set up Inbox link, lock screen circular shows sparkles icon → garden://setup-inbox, lock screen rectangular shows 'Set up Inbox' headline as a Link. When on: original Shortcut + count UI.

Drafted v2 App Store listing copy (subtitle, promotional text, 4-paragraph description, keywords) emphasizing widgets, lock screen accessories, Garden Inbox Shortcut + Action Button capture, and the Inbox-as-gated-feature design — surfaced in chat for Chad to tweak before submission.
decisions: Inbox is an opt-in feature, not a default. Default categories on fresh install: zero. Users either install the Shortcut (which creates Inbox) or create their own categories via Manage Categories. Strict-gating choice over softer 'show but warn' alternatives — chosen explicitly by Chad after device-test feedback.

Optimistic flip on Settings install button tap is preferred over waiting for the intent's first run. Tradeoff: if the user taps Install but cancels in Shortcuts.app, Inbox appears in the chip strip even though the Shortcut isn't actually installed. Recovery: tap Install again, or just delete the Inbox category. This was chosen over forcing a 'no Inbox until you actually run the Shortcut once' workflow that would feel broken on first install.

Hide-don't-delete: when the gate is off, the Inbox category and any notes within it stay in the SwiftData store; we just filter them out of the UI. Toggling the gate back on restores the full state with notes intact. Deleting would lose data on a temporary toggle and complicate CloudKit sync semantics.

Two notification channels: .gardenInboxEnabled (in-process flips) and .gardenShowSettings (deep-link triggers). scenePhase=.active is the cross-process refresh path for when an external intent flipped the flag while the app was backgrounded.
Notification.Name extensions consolidated in ContentView.swift, not split per-helper. Avoids duplicate-extension surface area and keeps the URL/notification mapping legible in one place.

Widget Compose buttons route to garden://setup-inbox (not directly to Shortcuts.app) when the gate is off — this gives a controlled in-app onboarding instead of Shortcuts.app's 'Shortcut not found' error screen.
gotchas: SourceKit got cranky after the new InboxGate.swift file was added — cross-file diagnostics like 'Cannot find Note in scope' and 'Type Category (aka OpaquePointer) does not conform to PersistentModel' cascaded into multiple unrelated files. These are stale-index artifacts of Xcode 16's PBXFileSystemSynchronizedRootGroup not having reindexed yet. They cleared when Xcode opened the project. Real swiftc compile was unaffected.

Widget extension target imports Foundation + WidgetKit but NOT SwiftUI in some files. Don't reference Color.* tokens from a file that doesn't import SwiftUI — silent compile failure with confusing 'Type Color has no member' errors.

@AppStorage with a custom App Group store works but doesn't reliably observe cross-process writes. Use @State + manual refresh on scenePhase=.active + NotificationCenter for cross-process flag flips when the source-of-truth lives in shared defaults.
next_session_starting_point: v2 is now feature-complete WITH gating. Chad to verify on device: fresh install hides Inbox everywhere → tap Settings install → Inbox appears in chip strip + composer + widget count line → Shortcut Speak/Type captures land in Inbox → all paths consistent. Then: (1) CloudKit Console schema deploy (CD_Note recordName Queryable index → Deploy to Production); (2) review/tweak the drafted listing copy and submit v2; (3) share link with wife/friends. Backlog stays as-is. Cleanup carryover: trim CLAUDE.md §Session-End Protocol to link chad-wiki instead of inlining the body.

session: v2 polish — Inbox triage / bulk-reassign (PM late)
summary: Chad came back with a follow-up ask after the gating pass: he wants to manually 'sort' the Inbox by bulk-selecting items and reassigning them to other categories in one go — Inbox-as-purgatory triage, basically. Read his 'manually sort' as 'sort through' / triage rather than drag-to-reorder, confirmed with him, then built a Mail-style multi-select mode on the Notes tab. Single feature, three small surface changes plus one new component. The v2 App Store listing draft from earlier this session is on hold — Chad wants to revisit before submission, so didn't update the listing copy to reflect this feature.
completed: SelectionActionBar component (Views/SelectionActionBar.swift): bottom bar with '{N} selected' label + a 'Move to…' menu listing every category except the currently filtered one, plus a Manage categories… escape hatch. Capsule-styled like a chip; disabled state when nothing's selected or there are no other categories.

NoteRowView selection chrome: optional selectionMode + isSelected + onSelectTap props. When selectionMode is on, leading SF Symbol check circle (filled when selected, outlined when not), card opacity drops to 0.6 for non-selected rows, the per-row Edit/Archive button row hides, any in-progress inline edit cancels via .onChange. Selected card border thickens to 1.5pt and tints sageDeep.
NoteListView passes selectionMode + a Set<UUID> binding through to active rows; archived section is hidden during selection mode (don't bulk-move archived).

NotesView selection state + toolbar: @State selectionMode + selectedNoteIDs:Set<UUID>. Nav bar swaps based on mode — when off: leading gearshape (Settings), trailing Select text button + share icon (Export). When on: leading Cancel, no trailing actions. Composer view replaced with SelectionActionBar; chip strip dimmed and disabled (filter stays visible but locked). Bulk move fetches all notes, mutates categoryID for selected IDs in one save, calls InboxCountStore.refresh, fires UINotificationFeedbackGenerator success haptic, exits selection mode.
decisions: Replace the composer with the action bar instead of stacking. Composer + action bar both anchored to the bottom would compete for space; replacement makes the mode change feel decisive and means the keyboard never appears mid-triage.

Disable (not hide) the chip strip during selection mode. Hiding would lose the user's filter context ('I'm triaging Inbox specifically'); dimming keeps the mental anchor visible while preventing accidental filter changes mid-selection.
Selection scoped to active notes only — archived section disappears when selection mode is on. Bulk-moving archived items isn't part of Chad's stated triage flow; if he asks later, easy to add.
Move destinations exclude the currently filtered category. Moving an Inbox note 'to Inbox' is a no-op; same for any other filter. Reduces the menu surface to just useful destinations.
Default props on NoteRowView are non-breaking: selectionMode/isSelected/onSelectTap all have defaults so other call sites (e.g., ArchivedNotesSection if it ever uses NoteRowView) don't need updates.
Mail-style nav button over long-press as the entry point — Chad picked this explicitly. Long-press would be cleaner but less discoverable for the friends-and-family share that's coming.
gotchas: UIKit import needed in NotesView for UINotificationFeedbackGenerator. SwiftUI's haptic feedback modifiers are nice but the explicit generator gives finer control over success/error/warning variants without a state-tied trigger.

Hiding the per-row Edit/Archive HStack during selection mode is cleaner than disabling — disabling leaves grey buttons that look broken. The HStack is wrapped in `if !selectionMode` instead of `.disabled`.

When entering selection mode while a row is mid-inline-edit, the row's local @State isEditing needs to be reset or the user gets stuck with hidden Save/Cancel buttons. Handled with .onChange(of: selectionMode) inside NoteRowView.
next_session_starting_point: Same as the previous PM entry plus: verify Inbox triage on device (Select → multi-select → Move to → confirms one save, success haptic, exit). Chad has paused on the v2 listing draft — revisit when he's ready to submit. Code is feature-complete for v2 polish; remaining gates are still CloudKit prod schema deploy + Chad's listing review + actual ASC submission.
